description: Back-port the relation_types consumed by shipped pi-context derivations but missing from the packaged catalog packages/pi-context/samples/conception.json relation_types[] (the single source of truth): `task_gated_by_item` (already in live .context/config.json — catalog-only add) and `phase_depends_on` (in NEITHER catalog nor live config — add to BOTH; the config add via amend-config). Before adding `phase_depends_on`, confirm roadmap-plan.ts (which consumes it at :329/:505/:528) is shipped/reachable, not dead code — if dead, surface that as the finding instead of registering the relation. Update the samples-catalog.test.ts relation_type count pin (mirror TASK-061's 31→34 bump). Entry shape per existing catalog entries: {canonical_id, display_name, category, source_kinds, target_kinds}. NOW back-port slice of FGAP-094; mirrors TASK-061/TASK-033 (ship vocabulary in the catalog).
acceptance_criteria: read-samples-catalog lists `task_gated_by_item` and `phase_depends_on` in relation_types[] with the exact {canonical_id, display_name, category, source_kinds, target_kinds} shape (task_gated_by_item: ordering, tasks→*; phase_depends_on: per its roadmap-plan usage).

A fresh /tmp substrate (context-init → context-accept-all → context-install) carries both relation_types — the original FGAP-094 symptom resolved: a `task_gated_by_item` edge is writable on a fresh substrate (no write-time rejection).
Live `.context/config.json` carries `phase_depends_on` (added via amend-config); `task_gated_by_item` already present there is unchanged.
samples-catalog.test.ts relation_type count pin updated to the new total; build/check/test green; context-validate clean.
notes: Cancelled 2026-07-02: its basis FGAP-094 is superseded_by FGAP-102, and its catalog back-port payload shipped via completed TASK-075 (commit e1cf07f). The forward parity-gate work it fed lives in TASK-067 (PHASE-M2-CATALOG-PARITY), whose stale dependency edge on this task is removed with this cancellation.
